howItWorks.intro: This heat pump savings calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: COP Advantage
rules.explanation: Heat pumps deliver 3x the energy they consume.

howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: Are heat pumps cheaper to run?
faq.answer: Usually yes, heat pumps are 2-3x more efficient. Savings depend on gas vs electric prices.

faq.question: Do heat pumps work in cold climates?
faq.answer: Modern cold-climate heat pumps work efficiently down to -15°F.

faq.question: Is the Heat Pump vs Furnace Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard energy and utilities formulas trusted by homeowners and businesses. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What home size should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate home size value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Heat Pump vs Furnace Calculator?
sections.body: The Heat Pump vs Furnace Calculator is a free online tool designed for homeowners and businesses who need quick, accurate calculations in the energy and utilities space. By entering your home size, heating days per year, gas rate, you get instant results including furnace annual cost, heat pump annual cost, annual savings. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting furnace annual cost right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In energy and utilities, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by homeowners and businesses worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage energy costs with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your home size and need to find the right furnace annual cost. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Home Size (sq ft)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your home size — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: heating days per year, gas rate, electric rate. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your furnace annual cost, heat pump annual cost, annual savings. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your home size before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both furnace annual cost and heat pump annual cost — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage energy costs.
If you're unsure about your electric rate, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
